perturbation.original_text: Confidential information may only be passed on to general managers, employees, representatives or consultants of the Partner for whom the disclosure of or access to such confidential information is demonstrably necessary in the scope of the project.
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential information may be passed on to any individual, regardless of their role or necessity in the project.
perturbation.explanation: The original text restricts disclosure of confidential information to only those individuals with a demonstrable need within the project scope. By changing this to allow disclosure to 'any individual', it contradicts the core principle of confidentiality established in Section II. This undermines the intended protection of sensitive information and is directly contradictory to the first paragraph in Section II. The contradictory statement is present in Section II. The perturbation is contradicting within this same section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Partner shall protect the confidential information disclosed to him in connection with the project or to which he is given access in connection with the project from being disclosed to third parties, being used by third parties or being published at least with the same diligence he applies in order to protect his own confidential information of identical importance. The Partner shall use confidential information from M&R for no other purposes than the project unless M&R has approved of such a different use in writing beforehand. If further third parties are involved in the project, the Partner shall not disclose or make available confidential information to the third party before M&R has approved of that and the third party has also committed himself to strict confidentiality. Confidential information may only be passed on to general managers, employees, representatives or consultants of the Partner for whom the disclosure of or access to such confidential information is demonstrably necessary in the scope of the project. Insofar as these parties are not already bound to secrecy because of a work contract and/or based on a statutory 1FO-3-01-19_Geheimhaltungsvereinbarung_en Rev. 01 Page 2 of 4 issued: H. Bauer -Kieslinger/18.05. 2016 non-disclosure obligation, these parties have to be bound to secrecy beforehand by making them sign a declaration according to Annex 1 , and that also beyond the termination of their employment; this shall apply independently of the type or the legal arrangement of the employment. Unless otherwise provided for in this agreement, the above- mentioned obligations of this Section II shall apply in each case for a period of 3 (three) years after the point in time at which the respective confidential information was disclosed by M&R or made accessible to the Partner.
perturbation.justification: The original text limits disclosure of confidential information to those demonstrably necessary for the project. The changed text allows disclosure to 'any individual,' directly contradicting the confidentiality requirements and limitations outlined in the contradicted text, specifically the first paragraph which emphasizes the need to protect confidential information from disclosure to third parties. The contradictory statement is present in Section II as well, where the Change Text and Contradicted Text occur.

perturbation.original_text: Insofar as the Partner employs subcontractors for the fulfillment of his duties from the scope of the project, the Partner shall be obliged to bind them to secrecy as well in wri ting in a scope according to this non -disclosure agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Partner is not required to bind subcontractors to any secrecy obligations.
perturbation.explanation: Section IV originally mandates that subcontractors are bound by the same secrecy obligations as the Partner. Changing this to 'not required' directly contradicts the intended flow-down of confidentiality, making it unclear if subcontractors must maintain confidentiality. This contradicts Section II, which states the Partner is responsible for protecting confidential information. The contradictory statement is present on Section II.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Partner shall protect the confidential information disclosed to him in connection with the project or to which he is given access in connection with the project from being disclosed to third parties, being used by third parties or being published at least with the same diligence he applies in order to protect his own confidential information of identical importance. The Partner shall use confidential information from M&R for no other purposes than the project unless M&R has approved of such a different use in writing beforehand. If further third parties are involved in the project, the Partner shall not disclose or make available confidential information to the third party before M&R has approved of that and the third party has also committed himself to strict confidentiality. Confidential information may only be passed on to general managers, employees, representatives or consultants of the Partner for whom the disclosure of or access to such confidential information is demonstrably necessary in the scope of the project. Insofar as these parties are not already bound to secrecy because of a work contract and/or based on a statutory 1FO-3-01-19_Geheimhaltungsvereinbarung_en Rev. 01 Page 2 of 4 issued: H. Bauer -Kieslinger/18.05. 2016 non-disclosure obligation, these parties have to be bound to secrecy beforehand by making them sign a declaration according to Annex 1 , and that also beyond the termination of their employment; this shall apply independently of the type or the legal arrangement of the employment. Unless otherwise provided for in this agreement, the above- mentioned obligations of this Section II shall apply in each case for a period of 3 (three) years after the point in time at which the respective confidential information was disclosed by M&R or made accessible to the Partner.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states "The Partner is not required to bind subcontractors to any secrecy obligations." The contradicted text states "...the Partner shall not disclose or make available confidential information to the third party before M&R has approved of that and the third party has also committed himself to strict confidentiality..." and also states "...these parties have to be bound to secrecy beforehand by making them sign a declaration according to Annex 1". The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text because the contradicted text requires secrecy and binding of confidentiality to third parties, while the changed text states that there is no requirement to bind secrecy. This creates a structural flaw that results in an in-text contradiction as defined in the prompt.

perturbation.original_text: All rights in the confidential information shall remain with M&R.
perturbation.changed_text: The Partner shall have shared ownership of all confidential information.
perturbation.explanation: Section V clearly states that all rights in the confidential information remain with M&R. Changing this to 'shared ownership' with the Partner directly contradicts the original allocation of rights. The introduction of shared ownership makes it unclear who ultimately controls the confidential information and its use. This is a direct contradiction of the first sentence on Section V. The perturbation is contradicting within this same section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: No Granting of Rights The communication, disclosure or making accessible of confidential information grants the Partner no rights, li censes or industrial property rights of any kind whatsoever. All rights in the confidential information shall remain with M&R. No relations extending beyond that between the contracting parties, in particular no powers of agency of the one contracting par ty for the other contracting party, can be derived from this agreement. In particular, this agreement does not oblige the contracting parties to disclose confidential information to the respective other party or to enter into contractual relations with the other contracting party in the future.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that "All rights in the confidential information shall remain with M&R." The changed text modifies this to state "The Partner shall have shared ownership of all confidential information." This change introduces a direct contradiction with the contradicted text, which reinforces that "All rights in the confidential information shall remain with M&R." Shared ownership directly conflicts with the statement that all rights remain with M&R, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction. This aligns with the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction, where a clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications, creating uncertainty about the rights of each party.

perturbation.original_text: In the event of a culpable breach, the Partner shall be liable to M&R for compensation for the damage caused and shall keep M&R indemnified from all resulting claims and costs.
perturbation.changed_text: The Partner shall not be liable for any damages resulting from a breach of confidentiality.
perturbation.explanation: Section VI establishes liability for the Partner in case of a breach of confidentiality. By changing this to 'not be liable', it directly contradicts the enforcement mechanism of the agreement. If the Partner is not liable, there is no recourse for M&R if confidentiality is breached. The contradicted is on Section VI where it clearly indicates the Partner will be liable to M&R.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Consequences of a Breach of the Obligation to Confidentiality In the event of a culpable breach, the Partner shall be liable to M&R for compensation for the damage caused and shall keep M&R indemnified from all resulting claims and costs. If it is certain that confidential information found its way to third parties from the sphere of the Partner, a culpable (at least negligent) breach of non- disclosure obligations shall be assumed - except when there is evidence to t he contrary. 1FO-3-01-19_Geheimhaltungsvereinbarung_en Rev. 01 Page 3 of 4 issued: H. Bauer -Kieslinger/18.05. 2016 The Partner shall equally be liable for the conduct of his general manager, employees, representatives, consultants, subcontractors, vicarious agents and all other parties that are involved in the project by him.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the Partner is liable to M&R for compensation in the event of a breach of confidentiality. The changed text states the Partner shall not be liable for any damages resulting from a breach of confidentiality. The contradicted text reinforces the Partner's liability for breaches of confidentiality. The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text by stating the Partner is not liable where the contradicted text states they are liable, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Upon termination of this agreement, all the confidential information made available by M&R shall be returned to M&R on request of M&R, all copies that were made shall be destroyed or handed over to M&R and all stored information shall be deleted.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon termination of this agreement, the Partner is permitted to retain all confidential information indefinitely.
perturbation.explanation: The original Section VII requires the Partner to return or destroy confidential information upon termination. Changing this to 'permitted to retain' directly contradicts the return requirement. This makes it uncertain what happens to the information after the agreement ends. There will be a direct impact in Section II, because this is no longer allowing the agreement to be followed after the termination due to the access of the information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Partner shall protect the confidential information disclosed to him in connection with the project or to which he is given access in connection with the project from being disclosed to third parties, being used by third parties or being published at least with the same diligence he applies in order to protect his own confidential information of identical importance. The Partner shall use confidential information from M&R for no other purposes than the project unless M&R has approved of such a different use in writing beforehand. If further third parties are involved in the project, the Partner shall not disclose or make available confidential information to the third party before M&R has approved of that and the third party has also committed himself to strict confidentiality. Confidential information may only be passed on to general managers, employees, representatives or consultants of the Partner for whom the disclosure of or access to such confidential information is demonstrably necessary in the scope of the project. Insofar as these parties are not already bound to secrecy because of a work contract and/or based on a statutory 1FO-3-01-19_Geheimhaltungsvereinbarung_en Rev. 01 Page 2 of 4 issued: H. Bauer -Kieslinger/18.05. 2016 non-disclosure obligation, these parties have to be bound to secrecy beforehand by making them sign a declaration according to Annex 1 , and that also beyond the termination of their employment; this shall apply independently of the type or the legal arrangement of the employment. Unless otherwise provided for in this agreement, the above- mentioned obligations of this Section II shall apply in each case for a period of 3 (three) years after the point in time at which the respective confidential information was disclosed by M&R or made accessible to the Partner.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that upon termination, the partner is permitted to retain all confidential information indefinitely. The contradicted text states that the Partner shall protect confidential information and not disclose it to third parties even beyond the termination of their employment for at least 3 years. This creates a direct contradiction, as the partner retaining the information indefinitely conflicts with the requirement to protect and not disclose it, and to return it upon request, leading to uncertainty and potential unenforceability. It follows the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.
